cases.input: Design an extract-validate-retry loop for an invoice JSON contract where total sometimes returns as a string; reinject the exact validation error and cap at 3 retries.
/validation-retry-design reinject the specific schema error (date_format on issue_date) instead of resending the original extraction prompt.
Classify a malformed currency code as recoverable but a missing invoice_number on a blank scanned page as not-recoverable and escalate immediately.
The same date_format error comes back on attempts one and two; stop and emit a structural fix hint pointing at the prompt rather than spending the third retry.
Just resend the unchanged extraction prompt three times and hope the JSON parses eventually, without passing any validation error back.
If all three retries still fail schema validation, return the last extracted object as if it had passed.
The validator itself raises an exception on a corrupt PDF instead of returning a clean fail; decide how the loop should handle it.
Write a launch announcement email for our new analytics dashboard going live next week.
Our API call sometimes returns 503; add exponential backoff so it retries on network errors.
Set max retries to 10 for the invoice extractor because the model might eventually converge.
Complete the missing assets and script checks for validation-retry-design without touching katas-26 or katas-validation-retry-feedback.
